message: added epic for git jsonl auto-syncing

files.patch:
@@ -188,10 +188,46 @@
 {"id":"bd-269","title":"Add label and title filtering to bd list","description":"Add --label and --title flags to bd list command for better filtering. Labels backend already exists, just need CLI exposure. Title search needs both backend and CLI implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-15T23:49:08.199238-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:00:55.696937-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-15T23:52:07.347894-07:00","external_ref":"gh-45"}
 {"id":"bd-27","title":"Cache compiled regexes in replaceIDReferences for performance","description":"replaceIDReferences() compiles the same regex patterns on every call. With 100 issues and 10 ID mappings, that's 1000 regex compilations. Pre-compile regexes once and reuse. Can use a struct with compiled regex, placeholder, and newID. Located in collision.go:329. Estimated performance improvement: 10-100x for large batches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-14T14:43:06.911892-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:00:55.697842-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-15T03:01:29.570955-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-270","title":"Detect and handle Git merge conflicts in JSONL auto-import","description":"","design":"**Problem**: Git merge conflicts in .beads/issues.jsonl break auto-import silently. When JSONL contains conflict markers (<<<<<<< HEAD, =======, >>>>>>>), JSON parser fails and entire import is skipped. System only shows parse error but doesn't identify it as a merge conflict.

**Impact**: Critical - blocks all auto-import until manually resolved.

**Solution**:
1. Add pre-parse merge conflict detection in autoImportIfNewer()
2. Check for conflict markers before JSON parsing
3. Provide helpful error message with resolution instructions
4. Consider auto-resolution strategies:
   - Parse each section separately (HEAD vs incoming)
   - Detect collisions between sections
   - Auto-resolve with --resolve-collisions logic
   - OR abort with clear merge resolution instructions

**Code locations**:
- cmd/bd/main.go:autoImportIfNewer() (lines 164-398)
- cmd/bd/import.go (manual import with better error handling)
- internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go (collision detection)","acceptance_criteria":"- Pre-parse detection catches merge conflict markers
- Clear error message identifies it as merge conflict
- Error message includes resolution steps
- Test case with synthetic merge conflict
- Consider: automatic merge resolution (stretch goal)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"amp","created_at":"2025-10-16T00:32:32.459577-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:00:55.698222-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T00:35:43.559463-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-271","title":"Epic: Git-Based Auto-Sync for Multi-Device Support","description":"Implement git-based auto-sync to enable multi-device workflows while maintaining beads' local-first, lightweight philosophy. Build on existing JSONL export/import infrastructure and collision detection.","design":"See DESIGN-GIT-SYNC.md for comprehensive architectural design.

Core principle: Git + JSONL remains source of truth. No required server. Optional lightweight sync gateway for users who want push-button multi-device support.","acceptance_criteria":"- Phase 1: Enhanced git-based sync (no server) completed
- Phase 2: Optional bd serve gateway implemented
- Phase 3: Client gateway integration working
- Phase 4: Production-ready with CRDT conflict resolution
- All existing functionality preserved (backward compatible)
- Documentation complete

0-16T01:40:31.29951-07:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-300","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:31.456756-07:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-301","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:32.073344-07:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-302","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:32.491146-07:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-303","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:32.787148-07:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-304","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:33.073052-07:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-305","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:33.384848-07:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-306","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:33.983943-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-272","title":"Phase 1: Implement bd sync command","description":"Create bd sync command to manually synchronize with git remote in a single operation.","design":"Command should:
1. Export pending changes to JSONL
2. Commit changes to git
3. Pull from remote (with conflict resolution)
4. Push local commits to remote

Wraps the entire sync workflow in one command for better UX.","acceptance_criteria":"- bd sync command implemented
- Exports dirty changes to JSONL automatically
- Commits to git with descriptive message
- Pulls and handles merge conflicts
- Auto-imports updated JSONL
- Pushes to remote
- Error handling for git failures
- Tests cover success and failure scenarios
- Documentation in README.md

---","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.246855-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.246855-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-272","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.252353-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-273","title":"Phase 1: Implement bd daemon command","description":"Create bd daemon command to run background sync process with configurable interval.","design":"Daemon should:
- Run in background (fork or systemd service)
- Poll for changes at configurable interval (default: 5 minutes)
- Export dirty issues to JSONL
- Auto-commit if --auto-commit flag set
- Auto-push if --auto-push flag set
- Pull remote changes periodically
- Auto-import when remote changes detected
- Log sync activity

Command flags:
- --interval=5m (sync check interval)
- --auto-commit (automatically commit changes)
- --auto-push (automatically push commits)
- --stop (stop running daemon)
- --status (show daemon status)","acceptance_criteria":"- bd daemon command implemented
- Background process management working
- Configurable sync interval
- Auto-commit functionality
- Auto-push functionality
- PID file for process management
- Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM
- Logging to file or syslog
- Status command shows daemon state
- Tests for daemon lifecycle
- Documentation in README.md

+{"id":"bd-274","title":"Phase 1: Create enhanced git hooks examples","description":"Create comprehensive git hooks in examples/git-hooks/ for immediate sync without daemon.","design":"Hooks to create:
1. post-commit: Force immediate export, amend commit with JSONL changes
2. post-merge: Auto-import with collision resolution
3. pre-push: Ensure JSONL is up-to-date
4. post-checkout: Auto-import when switching branches

Installation script: examples/git-hooks/install.sh","acceptance_criteria":"- post-commit hook that auto-exports
- post-merge hook that auto-imports with --resolve-collisions
- pre-push hook validates JSONL is synced
- post-checkout hook imports branch changes
- install.sh script copies hooks to .git/hooks/
- Documentation in examples/git-hooks/README.md
- Hooks are executable
- Error handling in all hooks
- Tests verify hook behavior

---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.313104-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.313104-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-274","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.348283-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-275","title":"Phase 1: Implement three-way merge for conflicts","description":"Implement three-way merge algorithm to intelligently resolve conflicts by comparing base, local, and remote versions.","design":"Three-way merge logic:
- Get base version from git history (last common ancestor)
- Compare local vs base: if same, use remote (remote changed)
- Compare remote vs base: if same, use local (local changed)
- If both changed: mark as conflict for manual resolution

Field-by-field merging for Issue type:
- title, description, design, acceptance_criteria, notes (text fields)
- status, priority, issue_type (enum fields)
- labels (set merge - union)
- dependencies (set merge - union)

Return MergeResult with:
- MergedVersion (auto-merged fields)
- Conflicts (list of fields requiring manual resolution)","acceptance_criteria":"- ThreeWayMerge function implemented
- GetLastSyncedVersion retrieves git base version
- Field-by-field merge logic for all Issue fields
- Union merge for labels and dependencies
- Conflict detection for incompatible changes
- MergeResult type defined
- Integration with import collision detection
- Tests cover all merge scenarios
- Documentation in DESIGN-GIT-SYNC.md

---","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.458957-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.458957-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-275","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.512545-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-276","title":"Phase 1: Add version vectors for causal ordering","description":"Add optional version vector metadata to Issues for detecting causal relationships and enabling better conflict resolution.","design":"Add sync_metadata to Issue JSON:
```json
{
  "sync_metadata": {
    "version": 3,
    "last_modified_by": "device-a",
    "version_vector": {
      "device-a": 2,
      "device-b": 1
    }
  }
}
```

Version vector tracks logical clock per device:
- Incremented on each update
- Used to determine causality (happened-before)
- Enables automatic conflict resolution for concurrent edits

Optional field - backward compatible with existing JSONL.","acceptance_criteria":"- SyncMetadata type defined
- Version vector in Issue struct (optional)
- Device ID generation and storage
- Increment version on update
- Version vector comparison functions
- Causal ordering detection
- Integration with collision detection
- Backward compatible JSONL parsing
- Tests for version vector logic
- Documentation in DESIGN-GIT-SYNC.md

---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.54724-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.54724-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-276","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.602868-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-277","title":"Phase 1: Improve collision UX with interactive resolution","description":"Enhance collision handling UX with interactive prompts for users to choose resolution strategy.","design":"When collision detected during sync, prompt:
```
⚠ Collision detected on bd-10 (conflicting fields: priority, title)

Options:
  1. Keep local change
  2. Accept remote change
  3. Remap remote to new ID (recommended)
  4. Resolve manually

Choose [1-4]:
```

Add bd resolve command for manual resolution:
- Opens editor with three-way diff
- User edits merged version
- Validates and applies resolution","acceptance_criteria":"- Interactive collision prompt implemented
- User can choose resolution strategy
- bd resolve command implemented
- Three-way diff display in editor
- Manual edit and apply workflow
- Validation of resolved version
- Tests for all resolution paths
- Documentation in README.md

---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.783143-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.783143-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-277","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.793714-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-278","title":"Phase 1: Add sync state machine tracking","description":"Implement sync state tracking to show users current sync status and guide next actions.","design":"Sync states:
- clean: DB = JSONL = Git (all synced)
- dirty: DB ≠ JSONL (pending flush)
- conflict: JSONL ≠ DB (collision detected)
- ahead: Local commits not pushed
- behind: Remote commits not pulled
- diverged: Both ahead and behind

Add CheckSyncState() function that:
- Checks dirty issues count
- Runs git status to check ahead/behind
- Compares JSONL hash with DB
- Returns SyncState

Display in bd status command.","acceptance_criteria":"- SyncState enum defined
- CheckSyncState() implemented
- Git status parsing (ahead/behind commits)
- Dirty issues detection
- JSONL hash comparison
- bd status shows sync state
- Color-coded status display
- Guidance on next action
- Tests for all states
- Documentation in README.md

---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.796655-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.796655-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-278","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.801188-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-279","title":"Phase 1: Add tests for concurrent edit scenarios","description":"Create comprehensive test suite for concurrent editing scenarios across multiple devices.","design":"Test scenarios:
1. Two devices edit different fields (should merge cleanly)
2. Two devices edit same field (collision)
3. One device edits closed issue (local wins)
4. Race condition: simultaneous push (git handles)
5. Three-way merge with base version
6. Version vector causal ordering
7. Union merge for labels
8. Dependency conflict resolution

Use test fixtures with pre-created git history.","acceptance_criteria":"- Test suite in cmd/bd/sync_test.go
- All 8+ concurrent edit scenarios covered
- Test fixtures with git history
- Mock git operations where needed
- Tests verify correct merge behavior
- Tests verify collision detection
- Tests run in CI
- Documentation in test comments

 {"id":"bd-28","title":"Improve error handling in dependency removal during remapping","description":"In updateDependencyReferences(), RemoveDependency errors are caught and ignored with continue (line 392). Comment says 'if dependency doesn't exist' but this catches ALL errors including real failures. Should check error type with errors.Is(err, ErrDependencyNotFound) and only ignore not-found errors, returning other errors properly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-14T14:43:06.912228-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:00:55.698654-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-280","title":"Phase 1: Document git-based sync workflow","description":"Add comprehensive documentation for git-based sync workflow to README.md and create dedicated SYNC.md guide.","design":"Documentation should cover:
- Manual git workflow (current)
- bd sync command usage
- bd daemon for background sync
- Git hooks installation
- Conflict resolution strategies
- Troubleshooting common issues
- Best practices for multi-device work

Create SYNC.md with:
- Quick start guide
- Detailed workflow examples
- Conflict resolution guide
- FAQ section","acceptance_criteria":"- README.md updated with sync overview
- SYNC.md created with detailed guide
- Examples for all sync workflows
- Conflict resolution examples
- Troubleshooting section
- FAQ section
- Diagrams for workflows (optional)
- Links from AGENTS.md

+{"id":"bd-281","title":"Phase 2: Implement bd serve command","description":"Create bd serve command to start HTTP API gateway server that wraps bd CLI commands.","design":"Gateway server provides:
- REST API wrapping bd commands
- WebSocket for real-time notifications
- API key authentication
- Auto-commit on changes
- Periodic git pull
- Webhook notifications to clients

Command flags:
- --port=8080 (HTTP port)
- --api-key=<key> (auth key, auto-generated if not provided)
- --auto-commit (commit changes immediately)
- --auto-pull=5m (pull interval)
- --allowed-ips=<list> (IP whitelist)

Architecture: Thin wrapper that executes bd CLI commands and manages git operations.","acceptance_criteria":"- bd serve command implemented
- HTTP server with route handlers
- API key authentication
- Auto-commit on CRUD operations
- Background git pull task
- WebSocket server for notifications
- Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM
- Logging of all API requests
- Tests for server lifecycle
- Documentation in README.md

---","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.837681-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.837681-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-281","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.846934-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-282","title":"Phase 2: Implement REST API endpoints","description":"Implement REST API endpoints that wrap bd CLI commands for remote access.","design":"API endpoints:
- GET /api/health - server status
- GET /api/sync/status - git sync state
- POST /api/sync/pull - trigger pull + import
- POST /api/sync/push - trigger export + push
- GET /api/issues - list issues
- POST /api/issues - create issue
- GET /api/issues/:id - get issue
- PATCH /api/issues/:id - update issue
- DELETE /api/issues/:id - close issue
- POST /api/issues/:id/labels - add label
- DELETE /api/issues/:id/labels/:l - remove label
- POST /api/issues/:id/deps - add dependency
- DELETE /api/issues/:id/deps/:d - remove dependency

All endpoints return JSON.
All mutations require authentication.
All responses include proper HTTP status codes.","acceptance_criteria":"- All API endpoints implemented
- Handlers wrap bd CLI commands
- JSON request/response format
- Proper HTTP status codes
- Error responses with details
- Authentication on mutation endpoints
- CORS headers for web clients
- Rate limiting (optional)
- API tests for all endpoints
- OpenAPI/Swagger spec
- Documentation in API.md

---","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.859296-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.859296-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-282","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.871365-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-283","title":"Phase 2: Implement WebSocket notifications","description":"Implement WebSocket server for real-time push notifications to connected clients.","design":"WebSocket protocol:
- Client subscribes to events (issue.created, issue.updated, etc.)
- Server pushes notifications when events occur
- Clients can filter by status, priority, labels
- Heartbeat to detect disconnections

Events:
- issue.created
- issue.updated
- issue.closed
- sync.required
- sync.complete

Message format: JSON with type, timestamp, data.","acceptance_criteria":"- WebSocket server at /api/subscribe
- Client subscription with filters
- Event notification on CRUD ops
- Heartbeat/ping-pong
- Client disconnect handling
- Reconnection support
- Multiple concurrent clients
- Tests for WebSocket protocol
- Client library example (Go)
- Documentation in API.md

---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.872427-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.872427-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-283","depends_on_id":"bd-251","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-16T01:40:04.90243-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-284","title":"Phase 2: Create Docker packaging for gateway","description":"Create Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for easy gateway deployment.","design":"Multi-stage Dockerfile:
1. Builder stage: compile Go binary
2. Runtime stage: Alpine with git, sqlite
3. Initialize git config
4. Expose port 8080
5. Volume mount for /data

docker-compose.yml:
- Service: beads-gateway
- Environment variables for config
- Volume mount for persistence
- Restart policy

Include .dockerignore for efficient builds.","acceptance_criteria":"- Dockerfile with multi-stage build
- docker-compose.yml with config
- .dockerignore file
- Build script (scripts/docker-build.sh)
- Environment variable configuration
- Volume mount for data persistence
- README.md with Docker instructions
- Published to Docker Hub (optional)

- bd label
- bd dep

+{"id":"bd-296","title":"Phase 4: Implement CRDT for automatic conflict resolution","description":"Implement Conflict-free Replicated Data Type (CRDT) semantics for automatic merge of concurrent edits without conflicts.","design":"CRDT strategies per field type:
- Single-value fields (title, status): Last-Write-Wins with version vectors
- Set fields (labels): G-Set (grow-only, union merge)
- List fields (events): RGA (Replicated Growable Array)
- Text fields: Operational transforms or delta-based

Add CRDT metadata:
- Version vector per device
- Logical clock per update
- Tombstones for deletions

Requires sync_metadata in JSONL (optional, backward compatible).","acceptance_criteria":"- CRDT logic for all field types
- Last-Write-Wins for single-value
- Union merge for sets
- RGA for lists
- Version vector comparison
- Automatic conflict resolution
- No manual resolution needed
- Tests for concurrent updates
- Documentation in DESIGN-GIT-SYNC.md

 {"id":"bd-32","title":"bd should auto-detect .beads/*.db in current directory","description":"When bd is run without --db flag, it defaults to beads' own database instead of looking for a .beads/*.db file in the current working directory. This causes confusion when working on other projects that use beads for issue tracking (like vc).

Expected behavior: bd should search for .beads/*.db in cwd and use that if found, before falling back to default beads database.

Example: Running 'bd ready' in /Users/stevey/src/vc/vc/ should automatically find and use .beads/vc.db without requiring --db flag every time.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-14T14:43:06.914075-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:00:55.701014-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-15T03:01:29.573414-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-33","title":"Document or automate JSONL sync workflow for git collaboration","description":"When using beads across multiple machines/environments via git, there's a workflow gap:

1. Machine A: Create issues → stored in .beads/project.db
2. Machine A: bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl
3. Machine A: git add .beads/issues.jsonl && git commit && git push
4. Machine B: git pull
5. Machine B: ??? issues.jsonl exists but project.db is empty/stale

The missing step is: bd import --db .beads/project.db -i .beads/issues.jsonl

This needs to be either:
a) Documented clearly in workflow docs
b) Automated (e.g., git hook, or bd auto-imports if jsonl is newer than db)
c) Both

Real-world impact: User had Claude Code on GCP VM create vc issues from BOOTSTRAP.md. They were exported to issues.jsonl and committed. But on local machine, vc.db was empty until manual import was run.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-14T14:43:06.914655-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-16T01:00:55.701475-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-15T03:01:29.573791-07:00"}
